ambiguities.description: Vendor model (approved vendor list, categories, ratings, contracts, performance)
Requisition workflow (who can request, what information is required, catalog vs free-form)
Approvals and authority limits (dollar thresholds, dual approval, department-based, role-based)
Purchase order model (manual, generated, integrated with ERP, numbering, terms)
Receiving and matching (two-way, three-way, goods receipt, inspection, exceptions)
Budgets and cost centers (who owns, how allocated, how tracked, over-budget handling)
Contracts and master agreements (storage, renewal, compliance, amendment workflow)
Vendor onboarding and compliance (qualification, certifications, insurance, risk assessment)
Integrations with inventory, accounting, ERP, or payment systems
Audit trail and compliance (who can see what, retention, regulatory requirements)
Exceptions and emergency purchasing (bypass rules, after-the-fact approval, thresholds)
MVP process boundary — what procurement stages are in scope for first release

questions.question: What is the requisition workflow (who requests, what info is required)?
What approval rules and authority limits apply?
Are purchase orders required, and how are they generated?
What receiving and matching model is required?
How are budgets and cost centers managed?
Are contracts and master agreements tracked?
What vendor onboarding and compliance requirements apply?
Must this integrate with accounting, ERP, or inventory systems?
What are the emergency purchasing and exception rules?

assumptions.assumption: A requisition → approval → PO workflow is acceptable for MVP
Dollar-threshold approvals with single approver are sufficient initially
Manual PO generation is acceptable for MVP
Simple two-way matching (PO vs receipt) is sufficient initially

suggested_closures.decision: Procurement construction will start with requisition → approval → PO workflow using dollar-threshold approvals and manual PO generation.
suggested_closures.rationale: A conservative workflow with explicit approval gates preserves principal control over spend while allowing incremental automation.

seed_tasks.title: Define vendor model and approved vendor list
seed_tasks.transformation: Document vendor categories, approval criteria, and how the approved vendor list is maintained.
seed_tasks.evidence_requirement: Vendor model and AVL policy exist.
seed_tasks.review_predicate: Every vendor category has approval criteria; no vendor is approved without documented rationale.

seed_tasks.title: Define requisition workflow
seed_tasks.transformation: Document who can request, what information is required, and how requests are submitted.
seed_tasks.review_predicate: Every requisition has required fields and a submitter; no request is ambiguous.

seed_tasks.title: Define approval rules and authority limits
seed_tasks.transformation: Document approval thresholds, who can approve at each level, and escalation rules.
seed_tasks.evidence_requirement: Approval matrix and authority limit document exist.
seed_tasks.review_predicate: Every spending level has an approver; no purchase lacks an approval path.

seed_tasks.title: Define purchase order model
seed_tasks.transformation: Document PO generation, numbering, terms, and how POs are sent to vendors.
seed_tasks.review_predicate: Every PO has required fields and a delivery method.

seed_tasks.title: Define receiving and matching policy
seed_tasks.transformation: Document receiving process, matching rules, and exception handling.
seed_tasks.review_predicate: Every receipt has a matching rule; exceptions have a documented resolution path.

seed_tasks.title: Define MVP procurement boundary
seed_tasks.transformation: Explicitly list procurement stages in MVP and what is out of scope.
seed_tasks.evidence_requirement: MVP scope document exists with explicit inclusions and exclusions.
seed_tasks.review_predicate: Scope is achievable and every excluded stage is consciously deferred.

residuals.description: Vendor model and approved vendor list are not defined.
Approval rules and authority limits are not decided.
Purchase order model is not defined.
Receiving and matching model is not established.
Budget and cost center model is undefined.
Emergency purchasing and exception rules are undocumented.
